title: dactylorhiza latifolia subsp. occidentalis
intro: Dactylorhiza latifolia subsp. occidentalis, also known as the Western Marsh Orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to the western parts of Europe. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza latifolia subsp. occidentalis include relieving congestion, reducing inflammation, and providing antioxidant effects. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture, infusion, or dried herb for medicinal use, although it's essential to consult a healthcare professional before using it for therapeutic purposes.
